optimizations.bottleneck: Kernel launch overhead: 51.8% GPU idle time (1.75ms gaps vs 1.63ms execution across 130 launches, avg 13.5us gap/launch). CPU dispatch serializes every CUDA API call, creating per-kernel synchronization bubbles.
optimizations.transformation: Enable CUDA Graphs via torch.cuda.make_graphed_callables() or torch.compile(model, mode='reduce-overhead'). Replaces 130 discrete CUDA API calls with a single graph replay, eliminating all CPU-GPU dispatch latency. Requires static shapes and no Python control flow in the captured region — satisfied by this model at batch=16, spatial=56x56.

optimizations.bottleneck: Shared-memory-induced wave starvation: Kernel2 allocates 73.7 KB shmem/block with only 4 warps/block (block_dim=[128,1,1]). On Blackwell (~192 KB shmem/SM) only 1-2 blocks fit per SM, yielding 4-8 active warps vs 64 max. Occupancy locked at 8.1-8.6% across all 30 instances. This kernel is 44.1% of total kernel execution time.
optimizations.transformation: Force Inductor to emit a Triton GEMM instead of falling back to cuDNN for 1x1 convolutions. Set torch._inductor.config.conv_1x1_as_mm = True, or in the FX graph replace aten::cudnn_convolution nodes where weight.shape[-2:] == (1,1) with aten::mm after reshaping activations to [N*H*W, C_in]. Triton tiles to 64x64 or 128x128 with configurable shmem, avoiding the 73 KB per-block cuDNN allocation and enabling multiple concurrent blocks per SM. Alternatively, torch.compile(mode='max-autotune') will autotune tile configs.

optimizations.bottleneck: Zero tensor core utilization across all 30 depthwise instances. cuDNN's depthwise path (groups=C_in) does not decompose into a GEMM and never engages Blackwell tensor cores. Kernels are memory-bound at 47-73% DRAM peak with no arithmetic intensity to hide latency.
optimizations.transformation: Replace cuDNN depthwise dispatch with a custom Triton kernel: intercept aten::convolution[groups==in_channels] in the FX graph via a custom lowering and dispatch to a Triton kernel using float4 vectorized loads (128-bit transactions per thread). The Triton kernel can also fuse the BN+HardTanh epilog (see OPT-004), eliminating the DRAM round-trip at the conv-BN boundary. TC cannot be engaged structurally for depthwise (no large GEMM), but vectorized loads reduce effective memory latency.

impact.note: TC utilization remains 0 (depthwise GEMM is structurally rank-deficient); gains are purely from memory access efficiency

fx_nodes: aten::convolution → aten::native_batch_norm → aten::hardtanh (all DSC block stages)

observed_metrics.fusion_status: BN+hardtanh already fused by Inductor; conv output NOT fused

optimizations.bottleneck: Redundant DRAM round-trip at conv-BN boundary: Inductor correctly fused BN+hardtanh into single Triton kernels, but the conv output is written to global memory, then immediately read back by the BN kernel. At [16,C,56,56] fp32, each intermediate tensor is 3-13 MB. Across 30 blocks this generates significant avoidable DRAM traffic.
optimizations.transformation: Register a custom Inductor fusion pass that pattern-matches convolution + native_batch_norm_legit_no_training + hardtanh in the post-lowering FX graph and emits a single Triton kernel with BN/clip as the conv epilog. For the cuDNN Kernel2 path: after applying OPT-002 (Triton GEMM), the epilog fusion is straightforward via a tl.store with inline BN scaling and tl.clamp. For the depthwise path: combine with OPT-003's custom Triton kernel. If hardtanh(min=0, max=6), aten::cudnn_convolution_with_bias_and_relu is also a candidate for the pointwise path.

impact.note: Eliminates one full activation read + write per DSC block at the conv-BN boundary

observed_metrics.dtype: fp32 (inferred: no fp16/bf16 kernels appear in profile)

optimizations.bottleneck: FP32 arithmetic throughout: Blackwell tensor cores deliver 2x higher throughput on BF16 vs FP32. Kernel2 TC utilization of 18-45% reflects FP32 TC rate, not the higher BF16 rate. conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc is memory-bound; halving dtype width from FP32 to BF16 directly halves DRAM pressure.
optimizations.transformation: Cast model weights and activations to BF16: model.to(torch.bfloat16) before inference, or wrap the forward pass in torch.autocast('cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16). BF16 preferred over FP16 on Blackwell (same TC throughput, wider dynamic range, no loss scaling required). In the FX graph, insert to(torch.bfloat16) on all aten::convolution inputs/weights via a torch.fx.passes type promotion pass. Keep BN running_mean/running_var buffers in FP32 — cast only conv inputs and weights.

impact.note: Highest arithmetic impact item; should be combined with OPT-002 for maximum effect on Kernel2

assumptions: device_name is null in capture metadata; GPU assumed to be RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (GB202) with ~170 SMs and ~192 KB shmem/SM. Occupancy estimates will shift if SM count differs, but the root cause is independent of SM count.

Most kernel attributions use name_heuristic (confidence: low). Kernel2 entries use NVTX (confidence: medium). Install torch.profiler NVTX markers (with torch.autograd.profiler.emit_nvtx()) for high-confidence operator-to-kernel mapping.
CUDA Graphs (OPT-001) requires static shapes and no Python control flow inside the captured region. The profiled model at batch=16, spatial=56x56 satisfies this constraint.
BF16 cast (OPT-005) assumes inference mode. For training, BF16 mixed-precision requires GradScaler and careful handling of BN statistics accumulation.
hardtanh bounds not confirmed from profiling data. If min=0 and max=6, the ReLU6 path via cudnn_convolution_with_bias_and_relu is available as an alternative epilog fusion strategy in OPT-004.
